infobox.title: Ironclad Dominion
infobox.imageCaption: Ironforge, capital of the Ironclad Dominion

lead:
The **Ironclad Dominion** is the dominant military power in southern and central [[Aethelgard]], a disciplined state built on industrial might, military hierarchy, and the absolute authority of its ruling High Marshal. Founded in 1654 AE by the legendary **High Marshal Korrath the Unyielding**, it controls the most extensive network of [[Aether-Crystals]] mines and is the sole producer of [[Steam-Golems]] at industrial scale.

The Dominion's philosophy is one of *ordered strength*: it believes that without strong central authority, the inherent chaos of the world — magical and human alike — will destroy civilisation. This worldview places it in perpetual tension with the scholarly idealism of the [[Lumina Collective]] and the decentralised commerce of the [[Sky-Merchants Guild]].

content.content:
The Ironclad Dominion emerged from the **Iron Confederation**, a loose alliance of mining towns and forge-cities that began pooling resources around 1600 AE. The Confederation's members shared a common frustration with what they saw as the [[Lumina Collective]]'s monopolistic approach to [[Aether-Crystals]] research — knowledge they felt should be translated into practical tools, not hoarded in archives.

**Korrath the Unyielding** (1621–1697 AE) united the Confederation under a single military command through a combination of tactical brilliance and political ruthlessness. His formal declaration of the Ironclad Dominion in 1654 AE was accompanied by the seizure of three major crystal deposits, immediately establishing the new state as a resource power.

content.caption: Official portrait of High Marshal Korrath the Unyielding, 1660 AE

content.content:
The **Great Expansion** (1700–1850 AE) saw the Dominion nearly double its territorial control. The deployment of [[Steam-Golems]] in 1672 AE gave Dominion forces an insurmountable advantage in open-field battles. The northern campaigns against the [[Lumina Collective]] (1710–1730 AE) ended inconclusively, largely because Collective Crystal Sentinels proved capable of disrupting golem coordination by interfering with their aetheric cores.

The **Battle of the Rift** (1843 AE) is considered the turning point of Dominion expansionism. Attempting to seize the primary [[Great Aetheric Rift]] monitoring stations, a combined Dominion force of 40,000 troops and 2,000 golems was repelled by a coalition of Lumina Sentinels and Verdant Weavers — the first time the two factions had acted in concert.

content.title: The Iron Pact
content.content: Following the Battle of the Rift, the Dominion signed the Iron Pact (1845 AE), agreeing to a 50-year moratorium on offensive operations against the Northern Reaches. The moratorium expires in 1895 AE — a date that has generated significant diplomatic activity.

The Dominion is an **authoritarian meritocracy** in which advancement is determined by demonstrated competence and loyalty. The **High Marshal** holds absolute executive, legislative, and military authority, advised by a **Council of Marshals** representing the nine major industrial and military branches.

Social mobility exists but is tightly controlled: talented engineers and soldiers can rise rapidly, while those who fail or dissent can fall just as quickly. Citizens take mandatory service oaths at age 16, committing two years to military or industrial service.

The Dominion operates a **command economy** in which all major industries are state-owned or state-directed. Private enterprise is permitted at the small-business level but any operation employing more than 50 workers requires a state operating licence and a Marshal-assigned production quota.

The major economic outputs are [[Steam-Golems]] (exported to neutral parties including the [[Sky-Merchants Guild]]), refined [[Aether-Crystals]], weaponry, and heavy machinery. The Dominion's trade surplus is the largest of any faction, though this wealth is heavily concentrated at the top of the social hierarchy.

perspectives.title: Internal Dominion Perspective
perspectives.content: Official Dominion historiography presents the state as the natural culmination of Aethelgard's need for strong leadership, framing all territorial expansion as defensive actions against encirclement by hostile factions.

perspectives.title: Lumina Collective Perspective
perspectives.content: Lumina scholars characterise the Dominion as an oligarchy of military elites exploiting working-class citizens and despoiling the land's aetheric resources for short-term power.
